task: Unified all spoken announcements in the voice subsystem through a single `announce()` pipeline, changed the default voice from Samantha to Zarvox, fixed empty-text handling in the `speak()` function to prevent spurious subprocess calls, and researched cross-repo Claude Code session switching as a potential feature for askr.
discussion_summary: The voice subsystem had multiple entry points for spoken notifications, each using different voice configurations. Prior sessions refactored all call sites to route through a single `announce()` function and changed the default voice to Zarvox per user preference. A previous session discovered and fixed a bug where `speak()` did not guard against empty messages and would call `say ""` anyway; the fix adds an early return when text is empty. This session pivoted to research: the user identified a workflow pain point — switching between Claude Code sessions in different repositories requires stopping the current session and manually pasting context into a new one. Research confirmed this is an open gap (Claude Code locks `.claude/` config to session-start directory) and not solved by upstream tooling, making it a potential feature for askr to address.

accomplishments.what: Refactored all spoken announcements to use unified `announce()` pipeline
Verified all bare `speak()` call sites eliminated and routed through `announce()`
Changed default single-voice mode from Samantha to Zarvox
Confirmed test suite passes after voice refactor
Committed and pushed voice unification (00bd902) and default voice change (80d2625)
Fixed empty-text handling in `speak()` to skip subprocess call when message is empty
Added comprehensive tests for empty-string handling in `speak()`, `speak_signature()`, and `announce()`
Verified full test suite (173 tests) passes after empty-text fix
Researched cross-repo Claude Code session switching problem and confirmed it is an open gap not solved upstream

next_actions.action: Test announce() pipeline with all 3 voice modes (single-voice Zarvox, dual-voice Good News + Zarvox, signature mode) to verify Zarvox default is applied correctly across all modes
next_actions.why: Open goal from prior session; ensures the Zarvox default works consistently across all voice configurations

next_actions.action: Document voice subsystem API changes and migration guide for Zarvox default in README or VOICE_API.md
next_actions.why: Open goal from prior session; helps future developers understand the unified announce() pipeline and voice configuration

next_actions.action: Evaluate feasibility of cross-repo Claude Code session switching as an askr feature: design session state serialization, handoff mechanism, and context restoration across repository boundaries
next_actions.why: User-identified workflow gap; research confirmed it is not solved upstream; could significantly improve developer experience when juggling multiple repos

decisions.decision: Default single-voice mode uses Zarvox instead of Samantha
decisions.reason: User preference: Samantha sounds too similar to Siri; Zarvox is the preferred default

decisions.decision: All spoken announcements route through unified `announce()` pipeline
decisions.reason: Eliminates inconsistent voice configuration across quota warnings, permission prompts, session-done pings, and other notifications

decisions.decision: Empty prefix or body strings are valid signals to skip that part of the announcement
decisions.reason: Allows callers to conditionally suppress voice output without special-casing logic; `speak()` and `speak_signature()` both guard against empty text

decisions.decision: `speak()` function skips subprocess call when text is empty
decisions.reason: Prevents spurious `say ""` calls that waste system resources; aligns with the settled decision that empty strings are valid skip signals

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Keep Samantha as the default voice for single-voice mode
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: defaulting to samantha will make it sound like siri, let's default to something else no G, for me

relational_files.why: Core voice subsystem with announce(), speak(), speak_signature() functions
Comprehensive test coverage for voice pipeline, empty-text handling, and all voice modes
Should document voice subsystem API changes and Zarvox default migration
